ddls:
CREATE TABLE "customers" (
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each customer */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the customer (male/female) */,
  "education" TEXT /* Level of education of the customer (e.g., university, high school) */,
  "marriage" TEXT /* Marital status of the customer (e.g., married, single) */,
  "age" INTEGER /* Age of the customer */,
  "default" INTEGER /* Default status of the customer (0/1) */,
  "income" REAL /* Annual income of the customer */,
  "employment_status" TEXT /* Employment status of the customer (e.g., employed, unemployed) */,
  "credit_score" INTEGER /* Credit score of the customer */,
  "risk_level" TEXT /* Credit risk level of the customer (e.g., low, medium, high) */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the customer record was last updated */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version of the customer record for data integrity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "payment_history" (
  "payment_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each payment record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who made the payment */,
  "pay_1" INTEGER /* Payment status for the first month (-1/0/1/2) */,
  "pay_2" INTEGER /* Payment status for the second month (-1/0/1/2) */,
  "pay_3" INTEGER /* Payment status for the third month (-1/0/1/2) */,
  "pay_4" INTEGER /* Payment status for the fourth month (-1/0/1/2) */,
  "pay_5" INTEGER /* Payment status for the fifth month (-1/0/1/2) */,
  "pay_6" INTEGER /* Payment status for the sixth month (-1/0/1/2) */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* Date of the payment */,
  "overdue_days" INTEGER /* Number of days the payment was overdue */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the payment record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the payment record was last updated */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version of the payment record for data integrity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("payment_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_payment_history_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "bill_amounts" (
  "bill_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each bill record */,
  "customer_id" INTEGER /* ID of the customer who received the bill */,
  "bill_amt1" REAL /* Bill amount for the first month */,
  "bill_amt2" REAL /* Bill amount for the second month */,
  "bill_amt3" REAL /* Bill amount for the third month */,
  "bill_amt4" REAL /* Bill amount for the fourth month */,
  "bill_amt5" REAL /* Bill amount for the fifth month */,
  "bill_amt6" REAL /* Bill amount for the sixth month */,
  "pay_amt1" REAL /* Payment amount for the first month */,
  "pay_amt2" REAL /* Payment amount for the second month */,
  "pay_amt3" REAL /* Payment amount for the third month */,
  "pay_amt4" REAL /* Payment amount for the fourth month */,
  "pay_amt5" REAL /* Payment amount for the fifth month */,
  "pay_amt6" REAL /* Payment amount for the sixth month */,
  "due_date" TEXT /* Due date for the bill */,
  "payment_date" TEXT /* Date when the payment was made */,
  "overdue_days" INTEGER /* Number of days the bill was overdue */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the bill record was created */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Timestamp when the bill record was last updated */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version of the bill record for data integrity */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("bill_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_bill_amounts_customer_id FOREIGN KEY ("customer_id") REFERENCES customers ("customer_id")
)
